steps.title: Take the brain of a C57BL/6J...
steps.instruction: Take the brain of a C57BL/6J mouse according to approved ethical guidelines.

steps.title: Rinse the brain with ice-cold PBS...
steps.instruction: Rinse the brain with ice-cold PBS and embed it in OCT compound.

steps.title: Snap-freeze the embedded brain in 2-methylbutane pre-cooled with liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Snap-freeze the embedded brain in 2-methylbutane pre-cooled with liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Store frozen blocks at -80 °C until sectioning. Note
steps.instruction: Store frozen blocks at -80 °C until sectioning. Note: All steps should be performed on ice or at 4 °C to prevent protein degradation.

steps.title: Cut the frozen block into a...
steps.instruction: Cut the frozen block into a series of 10-μm-thick horizontal sections using a cryostat. Note: Pre-cool the frozen block at -20 °C for 1 h and pre-cool the cryostat machine to a temperature of -20 °C to -30 °C.

steps.title: Collect sections on PEN membrane slides
steps.instruction: Collect sections on PEN membrane slides.

steps.title: Store slides at -80 °C until microdissection
steps.instruction: Store slides at -80 °C until microdissection. B. Laser microdissection

steps.title: Set up the Leica LMD7000 system...
steps.instruction: Set up the Leica LMD7000 system according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Imagine the whole-brain slide using the microscope camera
steps.instruction: Imagine the whole-brain slide using the microscope camera.

steps.title: For S4P sampling, select eight adjacent tissue slices
steps.instruction: For S4P sampling, select eight adjacent tissue slices.

steps.title: Define two feature points in the...
steps.instruction: Define two feature points in the brain tissue slice for angle alignment across adjacent slices. Note: Feature points should be clearly identifiable anatomical landmarks.

steps.title: For each slice, set a different cutting direction with 22
steps.instruction: For each slice, set a different cutting direction with 22.5° variation between adjacent slices. Note: The 22.5° increment (8 angles) ensures sufficient angular coverage for accurate tomographic reconstruction, which can be adjusted as needed; improvements in reconstruction algorithms can also reduce the requirement for angles.

steps.title: Program the LMD system to cut...
steps.instruction: Program the LMD system to cut parallel strips of 300 μm width.

steps.title: Collect dissected strips in 200 μL of opaque adhesive caps
steps.instruction: Collect dissected strips in 200 μL of opaque adhesive caps.

steps.title: Verify successful sample collection under the microscope
steps.instruction: Verify successful sample collection under the microscope. C. Proteomic sample preparation (bulk sample)

steps.title: Add lysis buffer for the bulk...
steps.instruction: Add lysis buffer for the bulk sample to a mouse tissue in 0.5-mL reinforced polypropylene tubes with 1 and 3 mm ceramic beads.

steps.title: Homogenize the tissue using an OMNI...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the tissue using an OMNI Bead Ruptor Elite instrument (10 m/s, 2 cycles, 30 s on, 15 s off) and then put it in ice water.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000× g for 20 min at 4 °C and collect the supernatant into the protein LoBind tubes.

steps.title: Add digestion buffer for the bulk...
steps.instruction: Add digestion buffer for the bulk sample and incubate at 37°C, shaking at 1,000 rpm for 16 h.

steps.title: Acidify samples with formic acid to pH 2–
steps.instruction: Acidify samples with formic acid to pH 2–

steps.title: Purify peptides with self-made stage-tips with reverse-phase C
steps.instruction: Purify peptides with self-made stage-tips with reverse-phase C

steps.title: Dry samples in a SpeedVac and reconstitute in 0
steps.instruction: Dry samples in a SpeedVac and reconstitute in 0.1% formic acid. Note: For DIA library construction in this study, a comprehensive spectral library was generated using data-dependent acquisition (DDA) mode. In brief, peptides were prepared from a homogenized sample (mouse brain strips), followed by high-pH reversed-phase fractionation to increase proteome depth [20]. The resulting fractions were analyzed by LC–MS/MS to build a spectral library, which was subsequently used to interpret the DIA data acquired from the microdissected tissue strips. D. Proteomic sample preparation (microdissected strips)

steps.title: Add 10 μL of lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL of lysis buffer to each cap containing tissue strips.

steps.title: Invert and incubate for 2 h at room temperature
steps.instruction: Invert and incubate for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5,000× g for 2 min to collect lysate
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5,000× g for 2 min to collect lysate.

steps.title: Add digestion buffer for microdissected strips
steps.instruction: Add digestion buffer for microdissected strips.

steps.title: Purify peptides using the Ex kit...
steps.instruction: Purify peptides using the Ex kit according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: Spike with iRT peptides for retention time calibration
steps.instruction: Spike with iRT peptides for retention time calibration.

steps.title: Dry samples in a SpeedVac and reconstitute in 0
steps.instruction: Dry samples in a SpeedVac and reconstitute in 0.1% formic acid. Note: Digested peptides can be stored at -20 °C for up to one week. E. LC–MS/MS analysis

steps.title: Configure the Ultimate 3000 nanoLC system...
steps.instruction: Configure the Ultimate 3000 nanoLC system with an analytical column (25 cm × 75 μm i.d.).

steps.title: Set the flow rate to 300 nL/min
steps.instruction: Set the flow rate to 300 nL/min: start at 4% buffer B and 96% buffer A, followed by a stepwise increase of buffer B to 28% in 40 min, 44% in 5 min, 90% in 3 min, and stabilize for 3 min; then, decrease to 4% in 3 min and stabilize for 7 min.

steps.title: Operate timsTOF Pro in diaPASEF mode...
steps.instruction: Operate timsTOF Pro in diaPASEF mode with 22 × 40 Th precursor isolation windows (m/z 249–1229).

steps.title: Set collision energy ramp from 59...
steps.instruction: Set collision energy ramp from 59 to 20 eV across the ion mobility range.

steps.title: Inject 200 ng of peptides per...
steps.instruction: Inject 200 ng of peptides per sample in the “sliding window” order [20] or randomized order to minimize batch effects. Note: Further details can be found in the original work [20]. F. Data preprocessing

steps.title: Process raw DIA data in Spectronaut 16
steps.instruction: Process raw DIA data in Spectronaut 16.2 using default settings.

steps.title: Search against the Mus musculus Uniprot database (UP000000589)
steps.instruction: Search against the Mus musculus Uniprot database (UP000000589).

steps.title: Set carbamidomethylation as a fixed modification,...
steps.instruction: Set carbamidomethylation as a fixed modification, with oxidation and N-terminal acetylation as variable modifications.

steps.title: Apply a 1% FDR cutoff at...
steps.instruction: Apply a 1% FDR cutoff at the precursor and protein levels.

steps.title: Normalize protein intensities by non-zero median...
steps.instruction: Normalize protein intensities by non-zero median values in each sample (strips data). Note: A preprocessed spectral library generated using Spectronaut is available for users who wish to skip raw data processing and proceed directly to spatial reconstruction (available via iProX repository: IPX0007383000).

steps.title: Save preprocessed protein expression data (CSV format
steps.instruction: Save preprocessed protein expression data (CSV format: rows = proteins, columns = strip samples) and a design matrix indicating strip angles and positions. G. Spatial reconstruction with DeepS4P

steps.title: Install the required Python packages
steps.instruction: Install the required Python packages: torch==1.13.1, numpy, and scipy.

steps.title: Clone the DeepS4P repository from GitHub at https://github
steps.instruction: Clone the DeepS4P repository from GitHub at https://github.com/Ma-JC/DeepS4P.

steps.title: Load preprocessed protein expression data and design matrix
steps.instruction: Load preprocessed protein expression data and design matrix.

steps.title: Configure neural network parameters
steps.instruction: Configure neural network parameters: Input layer: 446 nodes Hidden layers: 2048 and 4096 nodes with ReLU activation Output layer: 7330 nodes

steps.title: Set loss function parameters
steps.instruction: Set loss function parameters: Reconstruction weight: 1.0 L2 regularization (λ): 0.001 Sparsity weight (α): 0.01 Smoothness weight (β): 0.1

steps.title: Train the model for 1000 epochs with the Adam optimizer
steps.instruction: Train the model for 1000 epochs with the Adam optimizer.

steps.title: Apply a Gaussian filter (σ =
steps.instruction: Apply a Gaussian filter (σ =

steps.title: for image smoothing and noise reduction
steps.instruction: for image smoothing and noise reduction.

steps.title: Generate spatial distribution maps for each protein. Note
steps.instruction: Generate spatial distribution maps for each protein. Note: Specific parameters can be adjusted according to variations in sample setup and other conditions.

steps.title: Expected output is a 2D spatial...
steps.instruction: Expected output is a 2D spatial map per protein (saved as .npy or image file).

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been used and validated in the following research article: Qin et al. [20]. In-depth and high-throughput spatial proteomics for whole-tissue slice profiling by deep learning-facilitated sparse sampling strategy. Cell Discovery (Figures 2–7, Supplementary figures S1–S15). Validation data include a comparison with immunohistochemistry staining for known regional markers (Figure 3a, Supplementary Figure S9), consistency with the gridding-based spatial proteomics approach (Supplementary Figure S2), reconstruction accuracy for marker proteins and spatially co-localized proteins (Figure 4), and reproducibility across technical and biological replicates (Figure 2, Supplementary Figure S4).
